title: Random generator gives repeated results
description:
### What happens?

random() gives repeated results even when run only 100k times and single-threaded.

In comparison, numpy gives unique results even when asked for 100M random numbers.

### To Reproduce

```sql
SET THREADS to 1;
SELECT count() FROM (SELECT DISTINCT random() AS a FROM range(100_000));
```
```
┌──────────────┐
│ count_star() │
│    int64     │
├──────────────┤
│        99998 │
└──────────────┘
```


### OS:

Ubuntu22.04

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.0

### DuckDB Client:

Python3.9

### Hardware:

i5, x64

### Full Name:

Soeren Wolfers

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_random (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO test_random SELECT i, DBMS_RANDOM.VALUE() FROM (SELECT LEVEL AS i FROM dual CONNECT BY LEVEL <= 100000);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT DBMS_RANDOM.VALUE() AS a FROM (SELECT LEVEL FROM dual CONNECT BY LEVEL <= 100000));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_random WHERE value IS NOT NULL);
key_bug_features: random() gives repeated results in single-threaded mode
